evidence_quotes.quote: an online only experience where you collected aliens and could customize them in order to clear areas of enemies, a cool concept that sadly ended when the servers were shutdown
Publishers and developers are contantly pushing these online only games with the intent of dropping support after a few years
it's being able to play the game in an offline enviroment or in private servers at anytime given that a purchase was made
the companies can implement changes because games even in the year 2000 (when dial up was a thing), games had an offline mode

paragraphs.text: Hello to the person reading this, hope you doing well. I'm a Portuguese citizen but i decided to write in english, pardon my mistakes. I think the 1st time where i was upset on how certain games were treated was with the game "Darkspore", an online only experience where you collected aliens and could customize them in order to clear areas of enemies, a cool concept that sadly ended when the servers were shutdown, so even solo gameplay was impossible, this showcases what not to do, even if you buy a copy of the game, it's pointless. Good examples that i can recall was "need for speed hot pursuit", a racing game that makes it clear when you lauch the game that it can't connect to servers and as such online functionality can't be accessed, still you can play the game offline, doing races, running from cops, being the cops and just exploring the open road. Publishers and developers are contantly pushing these online only games with the intent of dropping support after a few years and this can come very soon after launch if the game performs horribly, things such as social politics, divisive storylines, poor performance and in some cases being a bad game. No one is asking for the game to have online features forever, it's being able to play the game in an offline enviroment or in private servers at anytime given that a purchase was made, as it stands some companies simply turn the switch and all your investment is gone, you can't even revisit the game at a later point. The companies can implement changes because games even in the year 2000 (when dial up was a thing), games had an offline mode, even despite being advertised as an online experience, here's an example of the game "phantasy star online" for the dreamcast console: https://consolemods.org/wiki/Dreamcast:Phantasy_Star_Online_Connection_Guide#/media/File:PSO_Connection_Guide_14.png I hope the law is checked and i warn whoever is reading this that certain groups and certain CEOs are often trying to divert attentions to bring this law down, either by talking about other subjects to justify the end of support or placing ridiculous claims on who made the effort to have this law going forward. Good luck and ty.
